patch.value: MCP nested options accept unknown keys silently (pm_deps options.dep no-ops) — extend pm-qxwu top-level warning into options objects

pm_deps with options.dep=id=...,kind=... returns success without applying or flagging anything: deps is a read projection whose options schema has no dep key, and unknown nested option keys are dropped before dispatch. The pm-qxwu warning mechanism only inspects top-level tool args, so mutation-shaped options silently no-op inside any narrow tool whose contract does not list them. Agents then build on state they never created (exactly how the PR#580-lane impact regression was born in mcp-dynamic-package-actions.spec).

tools/call surfaces a warning (matching the pm-qxwu top-level mechanism) for every options key absent from the invoked action's contract; contract tables stay the single source of truth; regression test covers a mutation-shaped key on a read tool

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Origin context preserved: discovered during PR #580 when a test assumed pm_deps options.dep mutates. The pm-qxwu top-level warning mechanism (TOOL_DECLARED_KEYS at tools/call) is the intended pattern to extend one level into options objects using the per-action optional lists in tool-schema.ts as the contract source.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence (2026-07-18): tools/call now warns on every nested options key absent from the invoked action's contract, using the exact pm-qxwu mechanism (stderr + additive structuredContent.warnings, never rejecting). Contract tables stay the single source of truth via the new exported pmToolActionParameterKeys(action) accessor in sdk/cli-contracts/tool-schema.ts — the same composition the strict action-scoped schema builder uses (action + global transport params + mutation projection keys + required/optional). Narrow tools resolve their fixed action from the new NARROW_TOOL_ACTIONS map (HANDLERS now derived from it, no duplication); pm_run resolves options validation from its action argument; extension/package actions have no contract entry and keep arbitrary passthrough. Regression spec covers the exact original repro class (pm_deps options.dep mutation-shaped key on a read tool), near-miss suggestions (options.formt -> format), pm_run action resolution, and all skip paths. Live MCP-session proof: pm_deps options.dep warning surfaced verbatim in structuredContent.warnings.

patch.value: Contract-driven nested-option validation in mcp/server.ts: detectUnexpectedOptionKeys warns (warn-only, pm-qxwu style) for every options key absent from the invoked action's contract, single-sourced from pmToolActionParameterKeys
pm_deps options.dep (and any unknown nested option key on a contract-described action) surfaces an explicit warning instead of silently no-opping

Warning emitted on stderr and in structuredContent.warnings with did-you-mean suggestion and declared-key list; call still succeeds; extension/package actions unaffected; verified live in an MCP session and by regression spec

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Review finding: contract-only nested option validation conflates canonical action-schema keys with runtime-compatible nested wrapper grammar. Full coverage remained 100/100/100/100, but integration traces proved valid list/update/checkpoint/project options were incorrectly labeled as no-ops. Fixing on the existing canonical item before merge; no duplicate item created.

author: codex-root

patch.value: pm_deps options.dep and other ignored keys warn on stderr and structuredContent; update-many/close-many wrapper grammar and extension/package project selectors do not false-positive; targeted MCP tests, static gates, and all 5,863 tests pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage.
Unknown or ignored nested option keys warn without rejection; runtime-consumed compatibility keys remain warning-free.

Nested-option validation now composes canonical action-schema keys with an explicit action-scoped runtime compatibility contract, so ignored keys still warn while list/update/checkpoint/project wrapper options consumed by MCP dispatch remain valid.

message: Correct nested-option contract to distinguish ignored keys from runtime-consumed compatibility grammar

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Completion evidence: review found and fixed false-positive warnings for update-many/close-many wrapper grammar and extension/package project selectors. Added an exported composed nested-option contract used by MCP validation while preserving canonical action schemas. Verification: pnpm build; 24 targeted unit/integration tests; pnpm quality:static (including 100% docstrings and package boundaries); full coverage 305 files/5,863 tests with statements 38,560/38,560, branches 27,105/27,105, functions 7,837/7,837, lines 37,556/37,556; git diff --check clean.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Nested MCP option validity has two sources of runtime truth: canonical action parameters and action-scoped wrapper compatibility grammar. Warning logic must compose both explicitly; using strict action schemas alone creates false no-op warnings for values consumed before command dispatch.

patch.value: Fixed nested MCP option warnings to preserve runtime-compatible wrapper keys while still surfacing genuinely ignored options; targeted integration, static quality, and full 100/100/100/100 coverage pass.
